highlights: The AI landscape is increasingly focused on the operationalization and safety of agentic systems, with new runtime architectures and evaluation frameworks emerging to ensure reliable and trustworthy deployments.

Major companies are significantly investing in AI infrastructure and expanding enterprise-grade AI solutions, empowering businesses from startups to large corporations with advanced tools and agent platforms.

Developers are gaining access to sophisticated tools and techniques for scalable LLM orchestration, precise model control, and efficient AI tool discovery, alongside advancements in training stability and constrained decoding.

agents.title: Introducing OpenAI Presence: Enterprise AI Agent Platform
agents.summary: OpenAI launched Presence, a robust enterprise AI agent platform enabling organizations to deploy trusted voice and chat agents for diverse customer and internal workflows, providing a ready-to-use solution for business automation.

agents.title: Expanding Managed Agents in Gemini API: Background Tasks, Remote MCP, and More
agents.summary: Google is enhancing Managed Agents in the Gemini API with new capabilities, including background tasks and remote Multi-Context Processing (MCP), allowing developers to build more reliable and production-ready AI agents.

agents.title: Phionyx: Deterministic AI Runtime Architecture with Structured State Management and Pre-Response Governance
agents.summary: Phionyx introduces a deterministic AI runtime architecture for agents, treating LLM outputs as noisy sensor measurements and enforcing reproducible state evolution via a structured state vector. This enables auditability, governance, and architectural privacy, crucial for reliable agent deployment.

models.title: S2T-RLHF: Hierarchical Credit Assignment for Stable Preference-Based RLHF
models.summary: S2T-RLHF proposes a sentence-to-token reward decomposition framework that improves the training stability and robustness of preference-based RLHF, addressing the challenge of ambiguous credit assignment with a granularity-aware approach without requiring reward-model retraining.

models.title: Probabilistic Concept-Aware Steering for Trustworthy LLM Inference
models.summary: This framework introduces Probabilistic Concept-Aware Steering (PCS) to guide LLM generation with concept-driven steering vectors and probabilistic strength calibration. PCS ensures trustworthy LLM inference by preserving task competence while enabling controllable, safety-oriented semantic bias.

models.title: Cross-Dialect Generalization Without Retraining: Schema-Derived Constrained Decoding for MLIR
models.summary: This research introduces schema-derived constrained decoding for MLIR, enabling smaller LLMs to match or exceed larger models in generating valid MLIR code without retraining. It provides a practical inference-time technique for developers working with extensible compiler infrastructure.

tools.title: BatchDAG: LLM-Planned Execution Graphs for Scalable Ad-Hoc Analysis Over Enterprise Data
tools.summary: BatchDAG is an orchestration layer where LLMs generate typed directed acyclic graphs for scalable, ad-hoc analysis over enterprise datasets. It dramatically reduces LLM calls and processing time by employing entity-aware batching and parallel execution, replacing complex hand-engineered workflows with natural language instructions.

tools.title: AI Tool Discovery at Scale: All You Need is DNS (ToolDNS)
tools.summary: ToolDNS is a radical framework that retrofits semantic AI tool discovery onto the Domain Name System, transforming complex semantic searches into lightweight, O(log N) name resolutions. This decentralized approach slashes discovery latency and search space, critical for the coming era of autonomous AI agents.

tools.title: PEARL: Solver-in-the-Loop Interactive Optimization Modeling from Natural Language
tools.summary: PEARL is a system for interactive optimization modeling that integrates Python execution and mathematical programming solvers directly into the LLM loop. It enables iterative model revision based on solver feedback, significantly improving verified solve rates for complex optimization tasks.

companies.title: Introducing the ChatGPT for small business program
companies.summary: OpenAI launched a program to empower small businesses with ChatGPT Work, helping entrepreneurs build AI skills, automate tasks, and leverage AI for growth. This initiative expands access to advanced AI capabilities for a crucial economic sector.

companies.title: Building AI infrastructure with the Effingham County community
companies.summary: OpenAI announced Project Camellia, a significant investment in AI infrastructure in Effingham County, Georgia. This commitment includes responsible energy use, community investment, job creation, and access to advanced AI, signaling a foundational expansion for future AI capabilities.

companies.title: New research shows how AMIE, our medical AI, could help manage health conditions.
companies.summary: Google's research in 'Nature' demonstrates that their conversational AI system, AMIE, can match primary care physicians in managing complex health conditions. This highlights Google's advancements in high-stakes AI applications and potential for significant impact in healthcare.

learning.title: How sales teams use ChatGPT Work
learning.summary: This guide from OpenAI Academy shows sales teams how to leverage ChatGPT Work for creating pipeline briefs, meeting prep, forecast reviews, account plans, and stalled-deal diagnoses, providing practical AI skills for business professionals.

learning.title: How data science teams use ChatGPT Work
learning.summary: OpenAI Academy offers insights into how data science teams can use ChatGPT Work to generate root-cause briefs, impact readouts, KPI memos, and dashboard specifications, streamlining analytical workflows and improving communication.

learning.title: Ask an AI expert: What exactly is the full stack?
learning.summary: A Google expert explains the concept of a 'full-stack approach' to AI, providing foundational knowledge for developers and strategists on why this comprehensive methodology is critical for successful AI development.
